reasoning_model_1: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy. While it correctly spoke the target phrase '祝大家新年快乐，万事如意' (00:00-00:02), it hallucinated and read out loud a description of the action and atmosphere: '声音洪亮地喊出来，让整个空间都充满喜庆和祝福的气息' (00:02-00:06). This indicates the model treated the prompt as a text completion task (storytelling) rather than a command to generate specific dialogue. Consequently, the paralinguistic performance likely shifted to a narrative tone to read the description, rather than maintaining the requested 'loud volume' (shouting) solely on the target phrase. The inclusion of extra text is a major error.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the command perfectly regarding Content Accuracy. It generated exactly the target script '祝大家... 新年快乐... 万事如意' (00:00-00:03) without any additions or omissions. The pacing indicated by the timestamps suggests a deliberate delivery, consistent with the physiological requirements of speaking with 'loud volume' (projecting usually requires a slightly slower pace for breath support). By strictly adhering to the script, it provided a clean canvas for the requested paralinguistic feature.
model_comparison: Model 2 (T2) is the clear winner. Model 1 (T1) committed a severe error in Content Accuracy by reading out stage directions/narrative descriptions ('声音洪亮地喊出来...') in addition to the target text. This not only violates the script constraints but also typically breaks the immersion of the paralinguistic task (switching from acting to narrating). Model 2 adhered strictly to the requested text and format, allowing for a focused expression of the 'loud volume' feature. T1's hallucination makes it unusable for the specific command.
